chore(governance): align agent workflows and refresh deps

Refine the govctl skill and reviewer prompts around scope, execution logs,
and durable learnings so work-item memory stays useful without becoming
normative authority.

Refresh dependency versions in Cargo.toml and Cargo.lock, including the
jsonschema and strum bumps required by the current build.

feat(plugin): distribute govctl as Claude Code plugin

Add marketplace.json, hooks, init skill, and ADR-0033 for packaging
govctl's agent integration as a Claude Code plugin. The .claude/
directory serves as both project-level config and plugin content.

- .claude-plugin/marketplace.json with source: "./.claude"
- SessionStart hook for govctl status context injection
- Stop hook for govctl check gate
- /govctl:init skill for first-time binary + project setup
- ADR-0033 documenting the plugin design decision
- WI-2026-03-04-001 tracking the work
docs(skills): add rendering rules to prevent agents from duplicating auto-generated headings and metadata
docs(readme): restructure as landing page with AI agent integration section
docs: update README, CLAUDE.md, and guides for migrate skill, nested editing, and current CLI
fix(publish): include gov/schema and skill subdirectories in cargo package
chore: merge new changes into 0.5.3 [skip ci]
fix(changelog): normalize canonical prefixes to verb form (add/fix/change/remove/deprecate)
feat(changelog): accept conventional-commit prefixes (feat, refactor, test, docs, etc.) as aliases
refactor(skills): add reviewer gates and trim inline commit syntax via skill composition
chore(work): complete WI-2026-03-03-001 — ADR-0032 migrate skill
feat(skills): implement /migrate skill for existing project adoption (ADR-0032)
chore(work): activate WI-2026-03-03-001 for ADR-0032 migrate skill
docs(adr): draft ADR-0032 migration skill for existing projects
fix(skills): remove repo-specific just pre-commit from bundled gov skill
docs(skills): add missing CLI commands and fix process gaps in gov, wi-writer, adr-writer
docs: update schema and guides for current model (journal, notes, alternatives, categories)
refactor: extract CLI definitions from main.rs into cli.rs
refactor(edit): consolidate edit cluster into cmd/edit/ directory module

fix(edit): address code review smells in V2 edit engine

- Fix bug: remove_nested_root_item now uses SSOT text_key for display
  text (journal entries show "content", not "<item>")
- Add text_key to SSOT nested rules (schema, codegen, runtime struct)
- Eliminate double root resolution: validate_nested_path takes rule param
- Use nested_field_supports_verb helper instead of inline verb check
- Add Display impl for FieldPath, remove duplicate format_path fns
- Inline add_toml_simple_field into add_to_field, remove V1 fn-pointer
- Collapse repeated write-back pattern in remove_toml_field
- Split set_json_field into set_rfc_field/set_clause_field, remove
  clippy::too_many_arguments suppression and post_update closure
chore(work): complete WI-2026-03-01-001 — ADR-0031 edit engine migration

feat(edit): implement ADR-0031 Phases 2-4 unified SSOT-driven edit engine

Replace V1 manual dispatch with V2 SSOT-driven operations per [[ADR-0031]]:

- Add content_path to SSOT nested rules (edit-ops.json + schema)
- Add generic nested field operations to edit_runtime.rs
  (get/set/add/remove for nested paths, all SSOT-driven)
- Replace per-artifact nested handlers in edit.rs with V2 runtime calls
- Remove ~640 lines of V1 dispatch code:
  - define_object_scalar_access! macro and all instances
  - adr_nested_get/set/add/remove_alternatives handlers
  - work_nested_get/set/remove_dispatch handlers
  - WorkNestedObjectSpec, nested helper functions
  - ensure_nested_depth, ensure_nested_field_for_verb
- Update 6 snapshot tests for V2 error messages and rendering
- All field-level operations now share one semantic engine
  (JSON and TOML use same pipeline per ADR-0031 completion criteria)
chore(work): activate WI-2026-03-01-001 for ADR-0031 edit engine migration
fix(edit): suppress clippy too_many_arguments and type_complexity warnings
feat(edit): implement ADR-0029/0030/0031 unified path edit engine

feat(cli): add --scope for journal and --pro/--con/--reject-reason for alternatives

- Add --scope option to work add for journal entries (per ADR-0026)
- Add --pro, --con, --reject-reason options to adr add for alternatives (per ADR-0027)
- --pro and --con can be specified multiple times to add multiple pros/cons
- If --reject-reason is provided, status is automatically set to rejected

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore(work): complete WI-2026-02-25-002

the purpose of journal vs notes vs description per ADR-0026.

Document alternatives pros/cons/rejection_reason fields in adr add
help per ADR-0027, noting they require direct TOML editing.

chore: bump version to v0.5.3
fix: changelog rendering
chore: release 0.5.2

fix(cli): wire up journal field in work add command

Add missing "journal" match arm in add_to_field() so that
`govctl work add <WI> journal "..."` works instead of returning E0810.

Also fix snapshot test instability: signature hashes embed today's date
via the spec, making them change daily. Normalize sha256 hashes to
`sha256:<HASH>` in test output so snapshots are date-independent.

Add test_work_add_journal integration test covering the new code path.
fix: bundle commit skill
chore(work): complete WI-2026-02-22-006
test(render): add unit tests for render_adr and render_work_item new fields
chore(work): complete WI-2026-02-22-005

feat(cli): improve help information for set/get/add commands

- Add VALID FIELDS section to work get/set/add/remove commands
- Add VALID FIELDS section to adr get/set/add/remove commands
- Add VALID FIELDS section to rfc get/set commands
- Add VALID FIELDS section to clause get/set commands
- Add EXAMPLES section for each command

This enables agents to understand available operations
without consulting external documentation.

Closes: WI-2026-02-22-004
chore: update changelog

- Add /commit skill for committing with govctl awareness
- Check work item status before/after commit

Benefits:
- Single unified format for all agent capabilities
- Portable across Claude, Cursor, and Codex
fix(work): update WI-2026-02-22-002 description to remove placeholder warning

feat(adr): extend Alternative structure with pros, cons, rejection_reason

- Add pros: Vec<String> field to Alternative
- Add cons: Vec<String> field to Alternative  
- Add rejection_reason: Option<String> field to Alternative
- Update ADR render to display pros/cons/rejection_reason
- Update adr-writer skill documentation

Implements: ADR-0027
Closes: WI-2026-02-22-002

feat(model): add journal field to WorkItem for execution tracking

- Add JournalEntry struct (date, scope, content) per ADR-0026
- Add journal field to WorkItemContent with optional serde defaults
- Update work.schema.toml with journal field definition
- Add '## Journal' section to work item render output

Implements: ADR-0026
Closes: WI-2026-02-22-001
chore: fix Cargo.toml version

fix(test): add --force to deprecate commands in tests

- test_scan_deprecated_rfc_reference: now correctly shows W0107 warning
- test_deprecate_normative_rfc: now correctly sets status to deprecated

Without --force, deprecate prompts for confirmation and fails non-interactively.

fix(test): enable source_scan in test_scan.rs for proper coverage

- Added enable_source_scan() helper to configure test projects
- scan.rs coverage improved from 2.05% to 59.20%
- Total coverage improved from 70.67% to 71.87%

Root cause: SourceScanConfig::default() sets enabled=false,
so test projects never exercised the scan code path.

chore(work): complete WI-2026-02-16-004 — test coverage improvement

Coverage improved from 53.98% to 70.51%

New test files:
- tests/test_describe.rs (10 tests)
- tests/test_scan.rs (10 tests)
- tests/test_lifecycle.rs (26 tests)
- tests/test_move.rs (10 tests)
- tests/test_edit.rs (28 tests)

All acceptance criteria met.

test(coverage): add tests for describe, lifecycle, edit, move, scan

- Add test_describe.rs: 10 tests covering describe command
- Add test_scan.rs: 10 tests for source code reference scanning
- Add test_lifecycle.rs: 26 tests for RFC/ADR lifecycle operations
- Add test_move.rs: 10 tests for work item status transitions
- Add test_edit.rs: 28 tests for field editing operations

Coverage improved from 53.98% to 70.51%

chore(work): activate WI-2026-02-16-004 for test coverage improvement

- Current coverage: 53.98%, target: 70%
- Priority 1: describe.rs (0%), scan.rs (2%)
- Priority 2: lifecycle, ui, edit, move_, command_router, parse modules

chore: add tests for init gitignore and custom paths

Add test coverage for init command:
- .gitignore automatic handling (create, append, no duplicate)
- Custom paths configuration (gov_root, docs_output)

Also add ensure_gitignore_lock_entry() to init process.

refactor(config): rename commands_dir to agent_dir

BREAKING CHANGE: config.toml must use `agent_dir` instead of `commands_dir`.
Old value `.claude/commands` should be changed to `.claude`.

The new design is cleaner:
- agent_dir points directly to the IDE root (e.g., .claude, .cursor)
- Commands, skills, agents subdirs are created under it
- No need to call .parent() to get the actual directory

Changes:
- Rename commands_dir -> agent_dir in PathsConfig
- Default changed from .claude/commands to .claude
- sync_commands now uses agent_dir directly
- Add tests for default and custom agent_dir

style: format function signatures and improve readability

- Multi-line function signatures for write_file, write_rfc, write_clause
- Multi-line assert! macros in test_lock.rs
- Update CHANGELOG.md with WI-2026-02-16-001 fixes
- Minor work item formatting
chore(work): complete WI-2026-02-16-001

fix(config): resolve relative paths based on project root

- Add Config::display_path() to convert absolute paths to relative for output
- Add optional display_path parameter to write_file, create_dir_all, delete_file
- Update all user-facing path outputs to use relative paths
- Add integration tests for lock behavior and display paths

Work item: WI-2026-02-16-001

feat(lock): implement concurrent write safety per RFC-0004

- Add file-based exclusive lock mechanism using fs2 advisory locks
- Lock file at gov/.govctl.lock acquired before any write command
- Default 30s timeout (configurable via concurrency.lock_timeout_secs)
- Read-only commands bypass lock, allowing concurrent reads
- Auto-release on process exit via GovLockGuard RAII pattern

Implements [[RFC-0004:C-CONCURRENT-WRITE]], [[RFC-0004:C-SCOPE]], [[RFC-0004:C-FAILURE-BEHAVIOUR]]
Per [[ADR-0025]] concurrent write safety design

commands rather than suggesting manual file edits.

refactor(cli): unify describe --format to --output for consistency

All output format flags now use --output / -o consistently:
- rfc list, adr list, work list, clause list
- rfc show, adr show, work show, clause show
- describe

Closes WI-2026-02-07-003

feat(cli): add show command for stdout rendering

Implements ADR-0022: agents can now use `govctl rfc show RFC-0001` to
read rendered content directly to stdout without writing files.

New commands:
- govctl rfc show <RFC-ID>
- govctl adr show <ADR-ID>
- govctl work show <WI-ID>
- govctl clause show <CLAUSE-ID>

Output formats:
- Default: markdown (human-readable)
- With -o json: structured JSON

Closes WI-2026-02-07-002

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

readme_html:
The Problem

AI-assisted coding is powerful but undisciplined:

  • Phase skipping — Jumping from idea to implementation without specification
  • Documentation drift — Specs and code diverge silently
  • No enforceable governance — "Best practices" become optional suggestions

The result: faster typing, slower thinking, unmaintainable systems.

Without govctl

Day 1:  "Let's add caching!"
Day 2:  AI generates 500 lines of Redis integration
Day 7:  "Wait, did we agree on Redis or Memcached?"
Day 14: Half the team implements one, half the other
Day 30: Two incompatible caching layers, no spec, nobody knows why

With govctl

Day 1:  govctl rfc new "Caching Strategy"
Day 2:  RFC-0015 defines: Redis, TTL policy, invalidation rules
Day 3:  govctl rfc advance RFC-0015 impl
Day 7:  Implementation complete, traceable to spec
Day 14: Tests pass, govctl rfc advance RFC-0015 stable

Quick Start

Claude Code Plugin

/plugin marketplace add govctl-org/govctl
/plugin install govctl@govctl
/govctl:init

The plugin provides workflow skills, reviewer agents, and enforcement hooks out of the box.

CLI Only

cargo install govctl
govctl init

govctl init creates the governance structure and installs AI agent skills into .claude/.

For complete documentation, see the User Guide.

AI Agent Integration

govctl is built for AI-native development. Install the Claude Code plugin or run govctl init to get workflow skills that any Claude Code / Cursor / Codex agent can invoke:

Skill Purpose
/gov <task> Complete governed workflow: work item, RFC/ADR, implement, test, done
/migrate Adopt govctl in an existing project: discover decisions, backfill ADRs, annotate source
/discuss <topic> Design discussion: explore options, draft RFC or ADR
/commit Smart commit: VCS detection, govctl checks, work item journal updates
/quick <task> Fast path for trivial changes (skip governance ceremony)

The plugin also includes enforcement hooks: govctl status runs at session start for context, govctl check runs at session end as a gate.

Every govctl operation is a single CLI call. No MCP server needed -- the CLI is the universal interface. Every shell-capable agent already speaks it.

What govctl Does

govctl enforces phase discipline on software development:

┌─────────┐     ┌──────────┐     ┌──────────┐     ┌──────────┐
│  SPEC   │ ──► │   IMPL   │ ──► │   TEST   │ ──► │  STABLE  │
└─────────┘     └──────────┘     └──────────┘     └──────────┘
     │                │                │                │
     ▼                ▼                ▼                ▼
  RFC must         Code must       Tests must       Bug fixes
  be normative     match spec      pass gates       only

Three artifact types, one lifecycle:

  • RFCs -- Specifications that must exist before implementation
  • ADRs -- Architectural decisions with explicit trade-offs
  • Work Items -- Tracked tasks tied to governance artifacts

govctl governs itself by its own rules. This repository is the first proof.

Who This Is For

  • Teams frustrated by AI "code now, think later" patterns
  • Existing projects that need to retroactively establish governance (/migrate)
  • Organizations needing audit trails for AI-generated code
  • Developers who believe discipline enables velocity

Not for "move fast and break things" workflows. Not for projects without review processes.

TUI Dashboard

cargo install govctl --features tui
govctl tui

Keymap: 1/2/3 to switch lists, j/k to move, Enter to open, / to filter, ? for help.

Community

  • Discord -- Questions, discussions, feedback
  • GitHub Issues -- Bug reports and feature requests
  • GitHub Discussions -- Design conversations

Contributing

govctl has an opinionated workflow. Before contributing:

  1. Read the governance RFC to understand the model
  2. All features require an RFC before implementation
  3. Phase gates are enforced -- this is the point, not bureaucracy

This workflow isn't for everyone, and that's okay. If you thrive in structured, spec-driven development, we'd welcome your contributions.

Star History

License

MIT

"Discipline is not the opposite of creativity. It is the foundation."
